title: pelargonium ternifolium
intro: Pelargonium ternifolium, also known as the 'Sour Fig', is a perennial shrub native to the Western Cape province of South Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The leaves and stems of Pelargonium ternifolium are used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to have various health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting immune function. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs, and is also used as a treatment for sore throats and mouth ulcers.
